pattern: Because AND distributes over XOR bit by bit, the XOR of all pairwise ANDs collapses to the AND of the two arrays' XOR sums.
hints: Look at one bit at a time: it survives only when an odd number of pairs have it set in both operands.
The count of such pairs is the product of how many elements set that bit in each array, and parity of a product depends on each factor's parity.
The whole answer equals (XOR of all of arr1) AND (XOR of all of arr2).

edge_inputs.description: single elements with no shared bits
even-sized arrays whose xor-sums share bits

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n1 = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    n2 = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    a1 = [rng.randint(0, 1000000000) for _ in range(n1)]
    a2 = [rng.randint(0, 1000000000) for _ in range(n2)]
    return [a1, a2]

stress.description: small arrays where the naive double loop is checkable
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n1 = max(1, min(size, 200))
    n2 = max(1, min(size, 200))
    a1 = [rng.randint(0, 1000) for _ in range(n1)]
    a2 = [rng.randint(0, 1000) for _ in range(n2)]
    return [a1, a2]
